---
title: ""
author: "Giorgio Arcara"
date: ""
#format: revealjs
format:
  beamer:
    theme: metropolis
    keep-tex: false
incremental: false
header-includes:
  \setbeamerfont{title}{size=\large}
  \usepackage{hyperref}
  \setbeamertemplate{footline}[frame number]
---

<!-- the section below is shown only if rendering is beamer from latex. (and format:revealjs is commmented) -->

::: {.content-visible when-format="beamer"}
```{=latex}
% TITLE SLIDES
\title{{\fontsize{15pt}{16pt}\selectfont Applied Psychometrics}:\\ studying subclinical psychopathologies\\ \vspace{1.5em} \raggedright 
\emph{4.2 - Validity: A priori}}
\author{Giorgio Arcara,\\ University of Padua \\ IRCCS San Camillo, Venice}

\titlegraphic{

\vspace*{7cm}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{Figures/LogoSanCamilloIRCCS_Unipd_alpha.png}
\begin{center}
\vspace{-2.2em}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{Figures/CC_license_3_0.png}
\end{center}
}
\date{2026}
\maketitle
```

## A priori validity

Is the validity that can be assessed even before data collection has started and pertain the theoretical robustness of the instrument.
\pause
\vfill

- **Face Validity**
- **Content Validity**
 
## Face Validity
\begin{center}
\textbf{\Large Face Validity}
\end{center}
\vspace{2em}
\pause
Is the extent the test _appears_ to measure what it is meant to measure

## Face Validity examples

A test on memory that ask participant to "remember" something will have satisfactory face validity.
\pause \vfill

A test on anxiety with items that ask how "anxious/nervous" you are will have satisfactory face validity.


## Notes on Face Validity
 
It is the less important type of validity

It is only assessed qualitatively (and often not reported) 

 

## Content Validity
\begin{center}
\textbf{\Large Content Validity}
\end{center}
\vspace{2em}

Is the extent the test covers all relevant aspect of a construct.

Typically it is first assessed ad item level, to assess if each item is relevant for the construct, and then reported at test level.

## Content Validity examples

A test on Obsessive Compulsive Disorder that include only items on obsessions but not on compulsions will have low content validity.
\pause \vfill

A test on anxiety with many items on depression will have low content validity, while one that cover all important aspects of the construct of anxiety will have high content validity.


## Notes on Content Validity 1/2
 
It is commonly measured with **CVI** (Content Validity Index), whose desirable value dependes on number of raters
 \vfill
 \centering
 _from Saiful Bahri Yusoff (2019)_
 
![](Figures/CVI_recommendations.png){width="80%" fig-align="center"}


## Notes on Content Validity 2/2

Very often Content Validity is not reported.

Content Validity as measured with CVI assess how well the items are related to the construct, but not how well the construct is covered.
